questions.question: In photography, contrast refers to…
options: The difference between tones, colours or light and dark in an image
The focal length of the lens
questions.reviewHint: Contrast is the degree of difference between elements - light vs dark, or colour against colour.

questions.question: What is 'tonal contrast'?
options: The difference between light and dark tones
The difference between two focal lengths
The difference between RAW and JPEG
The difference between two ISO values
questions.reviewHint: Tonal contrast is about light-to-dark differences, key in black and white work.

questions.question: Which type of contrast does black and white photography rely on most?
questions.reviewHint: Without colour, black and white images depend on tonal (light-dark) contrast.

questions.question: What is 'colour contrast'?
options: The visual difference created by placing certain colours together
The difference between two shutter speeds
The contrast between sharp and blurry areas
questions.reviewHint: Colour contrast comes from how colours interact - especially opposites on the colour wheel.

questions.question: Complementary colours are…
options: Colours opposite each other on the colour wheel
Colours next to each other on the wheel
questions.reviewHint: Complementary colours sit opposite on the wheel and create strong colour contrast.

questions.question: Warm and cool tones create contrast because…
options: They sit on opposite sides of the colour temperature range and visually oppose each other
They are always the same brightness
questions.reviewHint: Warm (orange/red) vs cool (blue) tones produce a pleasing, contrasting interplay.

questions.question: 'High-key' lighting produces an image that is…
options: Mostly bright with few dark tones
questions.reviewHint: High-key images are bright and low in shadow; low-key images are dark and moody.

questions.question: 'Low-key' lighting produces an image that is…
options: Mostly dark with selective highlights, often dramatic
questions.reviewHint: Low-key uses predominantly dark tones with a few highlights for a dramatic mood.

questions.question: A silhouette is created by…
options: Exposing for a bright background so the subject goes dark
Using fill flash on the subject
questions.reviewHint: Backlighting plus exposing for the bright background renders the subject as a dark silhouette.

questions.question: Contrast affects an image's…
options: Mood, depth and where the viewer's attention goes
questions.reviewHint: Contrast shapes mood and depth and helps direct attention within the frame.

questions.question: Which tool combines multiple exposures to handle very high contrast scenes?
questions.reviewHint: HDR merges several exposures to capture detail across a very high-contrast scene.
